facts_disclosed: UBC exchange cancelled — too expensive and would delay graduation

turns.content: so the ubc thing is dead. completely dead. im not doing it
Oh no -- that sounds definitive. What happened?

i finally sat down and actually looked at what a semester in vancouver would cost and its literally insane. flights alone are like $2000 return, accommodation over there is brutal because vancouver is apparently one of the most expensive cities on the planet, and then living costs on top of that?? no way. absolutely no way i can afford it. scrapping it completely

Vancouver is genuinely one of the most expensive cities in North America, yeah. International student housing plus flights plus daily living expenses would add up fast. Was there any exchange funding through your university that could have helped?

theres a small grant you can apply for but its like $2000 which would barely cover the flights let alone everything else. my parents cant help either, the restaurant isnt exactly swimming in cash. and honestly even if someone handed me the money tomorrow it would push my graduation back a whole semester. more tuition, more time not earning real money. its dead for two reasons. forget i ever mentioned it

That's a really clear-eyed assessment, even if it doesn't feel good. The financial barrier alone would be enough, but delaying graduation compounds it -- that's another semester of tuition and six more months before you're earning properly. Sometimes the right call is the disappointing one.

yeah and like... i ran the numbers properly for the first time. even being really conservative about it id need at least $15,000 for the semester. fifteen thousand dollars. i literally laughed out loud when i saw the total. that is not a number that exists in my life lol

Fifteen thousand is a lot of money, especially on a barista income alongside full-time study. That's a very real barrier, not a matter of just budgeting harder or cutting back on coffees. Did you look into it far enough to have already told anyone at uni you were considering it?

i mentioned it to my academic advisor a while back and she was keen for me to apply but she also doesnt have to pay for it lol. i havent formally applied or anything so theres nothing to withdraw from. its not happening. done. i need to stop torturing myself by looking at photos of the ubc campus because it is genuinely beautiful and it makes me sad

Good that you hadn't formally applied -- that makes it a cleaner decision with no paperwork to undo. Stepping away from the UBC Instagram is probably healthy at this point. How are you feeling about it emotionally, separate from the practical stuff?

i mean... i was genuinely so keen for it. studying marine ecology at ubc? campus literally on the ocean? would have been incredible. but im not going to bankrupt myself for a semester of vibes in canada lol. had a little sad moment looking at the ubc campus instagram but then i checked my bank account and that cured it real quick

The bank account reality check is the most effective cure for wanderlust. You're being practical about this, which is mature even when it stings. The door to international experience isn't closed forever -- it just doesn't make sense right now, mid-degree, with these costs.

yeah exactly. maybe ill travel properly after i graduate when i have actual income and dont have to worry about delaying anything. right now i just need to focus on the thesis and finishing on time. the canada dream is not happening and thats fine. onward

Post-graduation travel with your own money, on your own timeline, without academic logistics to navigate -- that's the better version anyway. Thesis brain is the right mode right now. Onward is the right word.

genuinely though i reckon in like two years ill be glad i didnt do it. right now it stings but future lily with a degree and a real job will not be crying about missing one semester in canada. she will be too busy being employed and eating food that isnt two minute noodles
Future employed Lily eating real food and not stressing about tuition debt is the version worth building toward. You made the smart call, even if it doesn't feel like it today.
